subject: [PATCH 1/4] mm: fix IOCB_DONTCACHE write performance with rate-limited writeback

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch's performance improvement is mainly due to a 23% increase in sequential write speed from 1179 MB/s to 1453 MB/s, and requested further investigation into this delta

Reviewer Ritesh (IBM) suggested that the performance improvement from 1179 MB/s to 1453 MB/s in a single write sequential test case could be attributed to reduced contention on xa_lock, and requested further investigation into this aspect.

Reviewer Ritesh expressed appreciation for the patch and acknowledged its benefits, but noted that it was a result of a bad merge during rebase and requested a v2 to be posted in the near future.
reviews.raw_body:
Sorry my bad.. I mis-understood this 2.5x delta at first.

So in a single sequential write case, what this patch is mainly
improving is from unpatched RWF_DONTCACHE (1179 MB/s) to patched
RWF_DONTCACHE (1453 MB/s) = ~23% improvement.

So the below theory which I was talking about was from this delta
perspective i.e. comparing unpatched v/s patched RWF_DONTCACHE mode.

---

That's right for comparison between buffered write against RWF_DONTCACHE.
But what I meant in above was for the improvement from 1179 MB/s to 1453
MB/s could be accounted to less contention on xa_lock on patched version
v/s unpatched version for single write sequential testcase.

---

Yes, thanks, that will be nice to have!

-ritesh

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch aims to improve write performance when using IOCB_DONTCACHE, a flag that prevents caching of writes. The issue arises from the flusher thread contending for the xa_lock when memory is tight, which can block readers and writers. To alleviate this contention, the patch limits the amount of flush work done by each writer and stays ahead of dirty limits, resulting in improved performance.
